description:
Offered: spring
This course covers architecture from the Neoclassical period to the present day. Topics may include: Neoclassicism, Gothic revival, the Arts and Crafts movement, Art Nouveau, Bauhaus, International Style, Postmodernism, sustainability and green architecture, industrial design, landscape design and urbanism. The sociopolitical context of modern architecture and the theories that shaped it will be emphasized.
Meets General Education Requirement(s): DP, FA
